name: Rhubarb Lemon Tofu "Pie" (Vlf Vegan)
recipeIngredient: 1 Granny Smith apple, peeled and cubed
2 Tablespoons whole wheat flour (or optional pie crust and crust crumble)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a rice cooker or pot, add a little water and the chopped rhubarb stalks. Cook covered for several minutes (if using range top cooking, be more observant as heat source may be hotter and add water as needed).
2. When the rhubarb is well done, carefully remove the cover (use an oven mitt) and add the cubed apple, strawberries, and 1/4 cup sugar. Cover and continue cooking for a few more minutes.
3. Meanwhile, puree the tofu in a food processor or chopper until very smooth. Add lemon juice, 2 tablespoons sugar, 2 tablespoons whole wheat flour, and process until well blended.
4. Line an 8-inch pie tin with parchment paper (or use a pie shell if preferred) and sprinkle lightly with a mixture of about 2 Tablespoons sugar and 2 Tablespoons whole wheat flour.
5. Spread the tofu mixture into the pie tin and bake at 400°F for a few minutes (until crust is done, if using a crust). Remove from oven and let cool.
6. Check the rhubarb mixture for sweetness (minimum of sugar) and adjust to preference. Pour through a fine mesh or cheesecloth-lined sieve and drain off juices without squeezing dry. Save juices for another use (perhaps for jelly).
7. Pour remaining rhubarb mixture over the baked lemon tofu. Refrigerate, or top with crust crumble and bake again until done.
The fruit mixture does not jell or thicken when a smaller amount of sugar is used, but it is preferred this way. The lemon tofu provides a sweet contrast to the tart yet sweet rhubarb and fruit filling.
From: Lu Bozinovich
